mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when policy is moving down.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is trending lower for 3 consecutive months, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional average by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth and housing starts both weaken together, the signal identifies broad demand deceleration that usually precedes lower corporate revenue expectations and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls month-over-month and HOUST is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weak-demand state does not outperform either proxy alone in out-of-sample IC, or if 60-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO after a soft patch indicates stronger industrial supply utilization and better cyclical earnings momentum, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if upside INDPRO crosses are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Sharpe is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains relatively restrictive, the market interprets policy as likely to stay tight, which raises real-rate pressure and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation exceeds the FEDFUNDS rate by more than 1.5 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/high-policy states do not generate negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the spread condition fails to add explanatory power over CPI or FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 indicates real output expansion and stronger aggregate demand, which lifts expected corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases in the latest quarterly print versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP expansions are not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal shows no positive Rank IC in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, labor-market tightening is confirming income growth and consumer resilience, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS is above its 3-month average and UNRATE is below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE condition does not outperform either labor proxy alone, or if triggered episodes fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts recovery after a prior slump marks a broad demand inflection that improves cyclically sensitive earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 consecutive months after being below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing-start rebounds do not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the signal reverses sign during recovery regimes.
